title: ferray-numpy-interop: AsFerray always copies — doc claims zero-copy borrow but implementation copies
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

AsFerray::as_ferray() in numpy_conv.rs is documented as: 'zero-copy borrow as a ferray ArrayView if C-contiguous, otherwise copy'. But the implementation always copies: 'let data: Vec<T> = py_arr.iter().cloned().collect()' for all three impls (Ix1, Ix2, IxDyn). It returns an owned Array, not an ArrayView. The trait signature returns Array<T, D>, not ArrayView. The doc says 'returning an ArrayView when the array is C-contiguous' but the code never creates a view. Either implement actual zero-copy via ArrayView::from_shape_ptr (for C-contiguous NumPy arrays) or fix the documentation.
